title: Q1 2026 Engineering Progress Report
description: Quarterly engineering report covering platform reliability improvements, API v3 migration, and mobile SDK launch for January through March 2026.
paragraphs: The engineering organization delivered 94% of planned commitments in Q1 2026, shipping the API v3 public release, completing the platform reliability initiative ahead of schedule, and launching the mobile SDK beta. Headcount grew from 42 to 48 engineers across three offices. This report details progress against OKRs, key risks, and the outlook for Q2.

The SRE team completed the migration from single-region deployment to an active-active configuration across US-East and EU-West. P99 latency improved from 420ms to 185ms, and the monthly error budget consumption dropped from 38% to 12%. Zero-downtime deployments are now standard for all tier-1 services.

We rolled out distributed tracing via OpenTelemetry to all backend services, replacing the legacy Zipkin integration. Mean time to detection (MTTD) improved by 62%, falling from 8.4 minutes to 3.2 minutes. The on-call runbook library was expanded from 23 to 71 entries, covering all critical-path failure modes.

The public release of API v3 shipped on February 18th, two weeks ahead of the original March target. The new version introduces granular OAuth scopes, cursor-based pagination, and webhook signature verification. As of March 31st, 34% of active integrations have migrated from v2 to v3. The v2 deprecation timeline remains set for September 2026.

Key adoption blockers identified through partner feedback include incomplete SDK coverage for Python and Go, which are scheduled for resolution in Sprint 14. The developer documentation site received 18,400 unique visitors in March, a 140% increase over the previous quarter.

The mobile SDK beta launched on March 10th with support for iOS 16+ and Android 13+. The SDK provides offline-first data synchronization, push notification routing, and biometric authentication wrappers. Beta participation reached 127 developers across 43 organizations by end of quarter.

The current beta does not support background sync on Android devices with restricted battery profiles, affecting approximately 15% of the Android user base. A workaround using foreground service notifications is documented, and a permanent fix is targeted for the RC1 milestone in April.

Six engineers joined the organization in Q1: two senior backend engineers for the data platform team, one staff SRE, two mid-level mobile engineers, and one frontend engineer for the developer portal. The overall offer acceptance rate was 83%. Two open senior roles remain unfilled in the security engineering team, with final-round candidates expected in early April.

The primary objectives for Q2 are the mobile SDK general availability release, completion of SOC 2 Type II audit preparation, and the launch of the real-time event streaming API. The most significant risk is the dependency on a third-party identity provider migration that is currently tracking two weeks behind their communicated schedule. A contingency plan using our existing auth layer is under review.
